verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a membrane-anchored ligand of the EGF receptor family. It is synthesized as a transmembrane precursor (proEREG) with an extracellular EGF-like domain on the outer face of the plasma membrane. The ectodomain is shed by proteolytic cleavage to release soluble epiregulin, but the membrane-anchored form is stably present on the outer leaflet via its single transmembrane domain, making it accessible to extracellular binders and capable of trans-interactions. The protein body (the ectodomain) is exposed on the outer face by virtue of its TM topology.
